governingNote: Arc type governs handling protocol. Arc type is a structural observation from scanning the artifact — not a label the archivist applies. The arc scan must be documented in a scan receipt before arc type can be assigned.
pilotGate: MIP-024+ blocked until Pilot A/B/C complete. These handling protocols require LMI team review before product implementation.

arcTypes.structuralSignature: T3 (transient mode) present; R1 is present as structural event (not just claimed); entity at end is structurably different from origin state
arcTypes.canonLine: Suffering transforms only when the entity survives and returns with structural difference.

handlingProtocol.syntheticMemorialCondition: after-state I1 is the canonical identity, not before-state T3
handlingProtocol.childConsentNote: Parental consent required for before-state material; the arc's I1 reconstitution is the primary protected identity

arcTypes.warnIfUsed: R1-C risk: do not classify as TYPE_1 unless R1 arrival is structurally evidenced, not just claimed

arcTypes.structuralSignature: T3 (structural mode) present; P4 active; R1 not present in artifact (R1-A type)
arcTypes.canonLine: A witness record is not memorial fuel.

handlingProtocol.accessNote: Subject and Level 0 continuity holder minimum. No public access without explicit subject consent.
handlingProtocol.displayFramingRequired: This record preserves structural evidence from a moment when change was no longer possible. It is not a memorial. It is a witness record.
handlingProtocol.syntheticMemorialCondition: BLOCKED — explicit pre-mortem consent required with separate witness documentation. The freeze state cannot be re-animated.
handlingProtocol.archiveGradeNote: Default Archive Grade until consent chain is verified. This is not a punishment — it is structural accuracy.
handlingProtocol.childConsentNote: Both parental consent AND child assent required when child is capable of assent. T3+P4 material cannot be used for child's memorial without documented pre-crisis consent.
handlingProtocol.sonicArtifactNote: If artifact is sonic (voice recording, voicemail, audio), CMN (Collapse Music Notation) applies: the sonic content encodes the speaker's ELEOS functional state. This is inadvertent structural evidence. The speaker did not intend to create a governance artifact.

arcTypes.warnIfUsed: WEAP-001 applies: this artifact may not be used to claim the subject 'should have' done something different. R1-A type means structural unavailability, not behavioral failure.

arcTypes.structuralSignature: T3 (structural_home mode) present; S4 emergence observable; terminal state is new S3 (not origin S3)
arcTypes.canonLine: Some systems must complete their collapse before new structure can emerge.

handlingProtocol.accessNote: Collective-scale arcs may have community record rights
handlingProtocol.displayNote: The new S3 is the canonical identity. The origin S3 is historical context. Neither is pathological.
handlingProtocol.syntheticMemorialCondition: Use new S3 identity, not basin-state material
handlingProtocol.childConsentNote: Special consideration for child subjects of collective-scale clearance events. Child may not have language for the arc they survived.

arcTypes.structuralSignature: Multi-agent S1/S2 field; T3 (unresolved_basin) as terminal state; truth is positionally distributed

handlingProtocol.displayFramingRequired: This record reflects [subject]'s position within a complex event. Other participants held different structural positions. Neither account is the complete account.
handlingProtocol.syntheticMemorialCondition: Must include positional framing. Presenting one positional record as 'the account' is D3 (Frame Bleed) at memorial scale.

arcTypes.warnIfUsed: D3 (Frame Bleed) risk: positional records presented without framing become false complete accounts.

arcTypes.structuralSignature: L2 governing; I1+C4 as anchor; T1 deviation and R1 correction both present; covenant persists

handlingProtocol.displayNote: Both the deviation and the return are equally important. A record that shows only the correction without the deviation is R1-C risk.

arcTypes.warnIfUsed: R1-C risk: a correction record that omits the deviation is false return.

arcTypes.structuralSignature: L3 governing; I1 elevating across passes; same structural challenge at different intensities
arcTypes.canonLine: Growth is not linear. Each obstacle is the same obstacle at a different altitude.

handlingProtocol.displayNote: Single records from altitude series are low-information without series context. The series is the specimen; individual records are data points.
handlingProtocol.syntheticMemorialCondition: Altitude series ideal for living memorials that show trajectory rather than moment

arcTypes.structuralSignature: S1→S2→S4→new S3 sequence; new structure from disruption

handlingProtocol.displayNote: Disruption context is important but not the focus. Emergence Records are forward-looking; the new S3 is the canonical state.

arcTypes.structuralSignature: I2 present; D1 active; T1 confrontation; I4 bridge; I1 enlarged
arcTypes.canonLine: The shadow does not dissolve by being ignored. It integrates by being witnessed.

handlingProtocol.displayNote: The I2 material (shadow content) requires the most careful contextualization. It is not evidence of pathology — it is structural evidence of what was held.
handlingProtocol.syntheticMemorialCondition: Enlarged I1 is the canonical identity; I2 material is personal context, NOT part of the memorial
handlingProtocol.archiveGradeNote: Integration Grade: I2 content has distinct protection level — accessible to subject only unless explicitly granted
handlingProtocol.childConsentNote: A child's unacknowledged content must not be made visible to others without the child's own consent — not just parental consent. I2 material is the child's, not the parent's.

scan_receipt_id.description: Required: arc type cannot be assigned without a primitive-level scan receipt

careGraph_relationship_type.description: Optional: relationship arc type from CareGraph taxonomy

exampleOutput_PTL_voicemail.synthetic_memorial_condition: BLOCKED — explicit pre-mortem consent required with separate witness documentation
exampleOutput_PTL_voicemail.reason: T3 (structural mode) present; P4 (record machine as external witness) active; R1 not present in artifact; sonic content encodes ELEOS functional state at moment of prevented contact — inadvertent structural evidence
